On BPG website notes:
HTML is solid. 271 elements, zero unclosed tags, zero mismatches. Heading hierarchy is correct with a single H1 and no level skips. No API keys or Stripe keys exposed in source. No innerHTML, eval, or document.write usage. No console.log debug remnants. Image alt text is present. All anchor links resolve to valid IDs. None of this is hard to fix. The mobile menu and the legal pages are the urgent ones. Everything else is polish that compounds.


High-priority:

No mobile menu. On screens under 600px, your nav links disappear. Anyone on a phone can only reach Contact and Cart. 

No Privacy Policy or Terms of Service. This is a legal requirement and compliance hawks and litigation hounds love to fuck with cannabis-related companies. 

No OG tags or social preview. When anyone shares your link on Instagram, LinkedIn, iMessage, Discord. They see a blank preview. You need Open Graph meta tags and a Twitter Card. 

No favicon. /favicon.ico returns 404. Small thing, but it makes the tab look unfinished. Your tree logo would look great here.


Accessibility:

These matter because ~8% of men have some form of color vision deficiency, and because screen readers and keyboard-only users exist in your customer base whether you see them or not.

28 font-size declarations below 12px. You're using 0.60rem–0.72rem throughout the site. That's roughly 9.6px to 11.5px. Even with your contrast ratios passing WCAG AA (your muted green on dark background hits 5.52:1, which clears the 4.5:1 bar), text that small is hard to read on most screens. Bump the floor to 0.80rem minimum.

3 form inputs without labels. The contact form's Name, Email, and Text area fields use placeholder text only; no <label> elements or aria-label attributes. Screen Readers can't announce to the user what these fields are. Placeholders disappear when you start typing, so sighted users lose context too.

Zero aria-label attributes. Cart button, tab controls, SVG logo; none have accessible names. Easy fix.

Seed/Clone tabs have no ARIA roles. The data-tab buttons work visually but aren't announced as tabs to assistive tech. Adding role="tablist", role="tab", and role="tabpanel" makes them work for everyone.

No skip-to-content link. Keyboard users have to tab through every nav item on every page load to reach your content.

Price drops are color-only. When an auction price drops below starting, the only signal is a color change to green. Color-blind users won't perceive the difference. A "REDUCED" badge or strikethrough on the original price fixes this.

SVG logo has no accessible name. No <title>, aria-label, or role attribute. Screen readers will either skip it or announce nothing useful.


UX:

Cart has no quantity controls. Buyers have to click "add" repeatedly instead of typing a number.

No <noscript> fallback. Your product data is JS-rendered. If JavaScript fails or is blocked, visitors see empty grids with no explanation. 

you@example.com placeholder still in the signup input. Looks like a dev leftover that should be a real example or just "your@email.com."

No light mode. Your investor page has a proper theme system with prefers-color-scheme and data-theme. The main site should adopt the same pattern.


Security:

Zero security headers. No Content-Security-Policy, no X-Frame-Options, no X-Content-Type-Options, no Referrer-Policy, no Permissions-Policy. Your site can be embedded in malicious iframes and is more exposed to XSS. If your hosting supports custom headers, this is a config change, not code.

Grow AI link opens without rel="noopener". The target="_blank" link to gpt.blackpinegenetics.com doesn't include rel="noopener noreferrer". Minor reverse tabnabbing risk.

15 inline onclick handlers. Not a vulnerability on its own, but it prevents you from ever adding a Content-Security-Policy header (which blocks inline scripts). Moving these to event listeners in a <​script> block future-proofs the security posture.


Performance:

No caching. Your server returns cache-control: no-cache on every response. Static HTML and CSS getting zero browser caching means every page load is a full round trip to nginx. Adding cache-control: public, max-age=3600 for static assets would speed up repeat visits noticeably.

27KB of inline CSS, no external stylesheet. Not terrible for a single-page site, but it means the browser can't cache your styles separately from your HTML. Every page load re-downloads all 27KB of CSS.

No canonical URL. No <link rel="canonical"> - could cause duplicate content issues if the site is accessible via www and non-www or http and https.

Yodel. Putting together a comparison page now - you'll be able to benchmark side by side. I'll build a secure port so you can hook your Qwen up stock and test it against the Consensus engines for proper A/B. Secure port, your keys, your data stays yours.

Consensus is model-agnostic middleware - sits above the inference layer so ROCm, sliding window, whatever you're running doesn't matter.

The engines effectively battle monolithic generality with advanced multi-model deliberation, sophisticated adversarial reasoning, and confidence gating so it actually says "I don't know" instead of hallucinating. More models in the topology = more resolution, not more noise.

Been building these for about 2 years, just got everything running in concert. Ready to let some folks stress test it.

Will have a link and a guide for ya shortly

Привіт, Давид, я працюю з бізнесами над побудовою та масштабуванням реклами у Google Ads та YouTube Ads, з фокусом не на лідах, а на реальних бізнес-показниках: CAC / CPQL / ROAS / LTV.
 Зазвичай ми підключаємось, коли реклама вже працює, але:
- результат нестабільний або “плаває”
- масштабування ламає окупність
- немає чіткого розуміння якості лідів і клієнтів
- аналітика з GA4, CRM і колтрекінгу не дає єдиної картини

Щоб було зрозуміло, як це виглядає на практиці, кілька прикладів наших проєктів з різних ніш:
1. E-commerce: продаж товарів через односторінкові лендинги без класичної товарної реклами (ROAS ~300%)
2. B2B (високий чек): генерація не просто заявок, а “цікавих” лідів з фокусом на CPQL
3. Логістика: відсікання дрібних і нецільових перевезень ще на рівні реклами
 Працюємо як performance-партнер: налаштовуємо аналітику (GA4, GTM, CRM, офлайн-конверсії), будуємо структуру кампаній і оптимізуємо рекламу під бізнес-результат, а не звіти.

Якщо у вас зараз або найближчим часом є задача стабілізувати чи масштабувати рекламу, пропоную короткий зідзвон на 15 хвилин, щоб зрозуміти, чи можемо бути корисні. Коли вам було б зручно?

SUBJECT: VP, Commercial Operations ∣ Orthopedic MedTech ∣ PE-backed ∣ Founder-led (<1 min. read)
CONTENT:
Hi David,

We’ve been engaged on a VP, Commercial Operations search for TriMed, a fast-growing orthopedic device company within the Henry Schein portfolio, based in Valencia, CA. This is not a narrow functional role. It’s a builder mandate.

The brief: partner directly with the founder/COO to professionalize and scale the operating backbone of a ~$50–100M business — supply chain, customer operations, contracts/pricing, IT/ERP, and operating cadence — while preserving what works in a long-standing, family-built culture.

What makes this compelling:
* GM-style scope without sales/marketing — true end-to-end commercial operations ownership.
* Build vs. inherit — ERP implementation, SOPs, KPIs, and decision cadence are ahead of the growth curve, not behind it.
* Founder-led → enterprise transition — success here is about influence, trust, and sequencing, not command-and-control.
* Real runway — role sits on the leadership team with clear expansion potential as the platform scales.
* Strong partners — engaged GM, supportive parent company, resources in place.

This is a fit for an operator who’s scaled founder-led or PE-backed businesses, enjoys getting into the details, and knows how to bring people along through change.

Location: Valencia, CA (onsite)
Compensation: Up to the low $200k’s base based on experience + bonus

If this shape of role resonates, happy to share more and learn how you’ve approached building operating systems in under-structured environments.

 If timing’s not right, a quick “not now” or a referral is always appreciated.

SUBJECT: Personal Assistant Role - Couldn't Ignore The Alignment
CONTENT:
Jay,

I missed your application window, but the alignment here is too strong to let that stop me.

I've spent my career as the right-hand to founders building hard things. At Good Day Farm, I freed leadership to focus on product while building the execution systems behind 4x growth. At Techs.Today, I ran operations across 11 countries for $450M in technical divisions. The pattern is always the same: I own the operational machine so you can focus on vision.

Reading about you engineering a car at 16 hit differently - I've spent 20 years doing the same kind of work. Built a '65 Mustang with a 302 swap, custom pre-runner, currently fabricating performance e-scooters in my garage. The way you think about mechanical systems - breaking them down to first principles, understanding how every component interacts - that's exactly the cognitive framework I bring to operational architecture. Not many people parse the world this way.

What struck me about your story was the protection instinct - using technology to extend human compassion. That's not corporate speak for me. My entire career has been service-oriented: handle everything so the founder can do what only they can do, with complete discretion in high-stakes environments.

I'm also building technical systems myself - currently architecting an AI consensus orchestration platform despite never having written production code before. Same first-principles approach, different domain. 

Application packet attached (cover letter, resume, technical supplement showing the mechanical/technical work). Based in LA, U.S. Citizen, ITAR-compliant, comfortable with whatever pace this demands.

The way you operate - multiple technical domains simultaneously, extreme intensity - that's not something I need to accommodate. That's already my rhythm.

Would love to have a conversation if you're still reviewing candidates. 

I genuinely think I could be a significant force multiplier for you, and maybe even a friend, too. 

Thanks sincerely for reading,

David Kirsch

Hi Delaney,

I'm reaching out about the Executive Operations Partner role at Demand.io. After reading the posting, I think I might be exactly what they're looking for - someone who's already building AI-native operational systems while having the founder support experience they need.

The role description caught my attention because it's describing what I've been doing my whole career, just at a much more sophisticated level. I've spent 20 years as the operational right-hand to founders - Stephen Rosenblueth, Good Day Farm, de Krown Enterprises - always being the person who turns vision into systematic execution.

What makes this particularly relevant is timing. I'm currently building a consensus AI platform that orchestrates multiple LLMs (GPT, Claude, DeepSeek) in my spare time. When I read about their AIOS approach and need for someone who can architect AI-native operational systems, it felt like they were describing exactly what I'm prototyping.

The cultural fit seems strong too. I've intentionally walked away from corporate environments because I can't compromise values for bureaucracy. Their anti-corporate, outcome-driven culture with profit-sharing represents how I already operate my own businesses.

I'm not going to oversell - I'd need to learn Notion quickly, and some of their specific frameworks. But I bring something unique: I'm not theoretically interested in AI-native operations, I'm actively building them. The systematic thinking that created my platform directly transfers to organizational excellence.

I've included a technical addendum with my application that shows the depth of this work. It's not just talk - there's actual systems architecture backing up the claims.

I'd be happy to discuss how this experience translates to what Demand.io needs. Do you have time for a brief conversation about the role?

David Kirsch

Hi David, I hope this DM finds you well. I’d like to meet with you to discuss the inclusion of braille labels on cannabis packaging as an essential step towards ensuring accessibility for all consumers. Braille labels empower visually impaired individuals to independently access information about cannabis products, promoting inclusivity and equal access. This initiative demonstrates a commitment to accommodating diverse needs and fostering an environment where everyone can make informed choices.
Incorporating braille labels not only enhances accessibility but also improves customer trust and satisfaction. Visually impaired consumers will appreciate the effort to make products accessible and user-friendly, leading to increased loyalty and positive word-of-mouth. This can create a ripple effect, encouraging other brands to follow suit in adopting inclusive practices, thus setting a new standard in the industry.
Moreover, braille labeling on cannabis packaging aligns with legal and ethical standards for accessibility. Many regions require accommodations for disabled individuals, and implementing braille labels ensures compliance with such regulations. Beyond compliance, this proactive approach shows social responsibility and a genuine dedication to diversity and inclusion. By prioritizing accessible packaging, the cannabis industry can lead by example, contributing to a more equitable society for all.
2Blind Hippie will make braille labels for your brand for about a dime each.

Hey Phil,

Please pardon the left-field LI DM... I know you probably get a ton of sponsorship pitches, but hear me out—I'll get right to the point; this one’s different.

I built Clutch Plug from the ground up as an anti-corporate, no-BS, straight-to-the-source cannabis brand. No gimmicks, no overpriced hype—just fire product, priced right, with full transparency. The problem? We put everything into making sure the foundation was solid, and now we’re sitting here with the whole operation ready to go—but no budget left for marketing.

We’re at that moment where the right push could mean everything, and if we’re being honest, you are the exact type of person who could make that push count. Your personality? Our people. Your audience? Right in our wheelhouse. I really don't feel there's a forced brand alignment here.

Instead of some boring paid sponsorship deal, we’d rather put our money where our product is and make sure you and your team never run dry. Every month, like clockwork, we’ll send you a fresh supply of product for free—no strings, no weird fine print, no quotas to hit. While we don't have much cash to throw around right now, we're open to exploring other opportunities as well. 

In return, we just need exposure.

Shoutouts? On-screen placement? Whatever feels natural in your content.
Casual mentions? We don’t need a whole ad read—just let it be part of your world.
Product in the background? Even subtle visibility helps.
If there’s a way we can sweeten the deal further, we’re open to that conversation too. We know time and effort matter, and we’re down to make sure this is worth yours.

If this sounds like something you’d be into, let’s talk. We’ll walk you through everything—who we are, how we operate, and why we’re not just another cannabis brand trying to throw money at marketing. We’re just like you—small team, built from passion, putting in the work, and just needing the right break to make it big.

Let me know what you think. If it’s not the right fit, no hard feelings—but if it is, I think we could build something dope together, in some capacity or another. 

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts - get at me anytime.


Best,

David Kirsch
Founder, CEO | Clutch Plug
805.341.5381
david@theclutchplug.com
www.theclutchplug.com


PS - Been following you since 2015 when I met my wife. Thanks for keeping us and everyone else phil'd in, brother!

SUBJECT: Doughbricks Pizza - CEO Role
CONTENT:
Hey there, Ilya. I know the job was just taken down, but I'd appreciate it if you could add this to the stack—it closed as I was writing the cover letter. Thanks so much.

With a track record of leading diverse teams to drive substantial growth and operational excellence, I am genuinely excited to apply for the CEO position at Doughbricks Pizza. At age 17, I became Domino's Pizza's youngest assistant general manager in history. Outside of Domnios, I have extensive pizza shop management experience from several other shops in Simi Valley, CA, before transitioning to Cannabis & Tech.

My journey through industries, from the objectively challenging cannabis industry to tech startups, has "built a lot of character," my ability to innovate and optimize operations, even in rambunctious, ambiguous startup environments, is incredibly strong. Razor-thin margins and managing the manufacturing of products regulated more than nuclear-grade plutonium have forged a nimble, resilient, and creative leader with the drive to get things done even in the most competitive environments.

As Vice President of Operations at Good Day Farm, I quadrupled revenue in 18 months by implementing lean strategies and 5S principles (among many other initiatives) and fostering a culture of excellence driven by pride through empowerment.

As a leader of Speak With A Geek's global operations team, I managed hundreds of employees across multiple countries, demonstrating my ability to manage and lead projects with distributed teams driving successful outcomes.

Doughbricks Pizza's vision of expanding nationwide and launching a frozen pizza line resonates well with my experience and personal business interests. I would be thrilled to be an integral force in propelling Doughbricks into a national brand. My experience in business planning, finance, and creative resourcefulness aligns perfectly with the role's requirements. Moreover, my background in the restaurant industry, coupled with a servant leadership approach, ensures happy employees.

Happy employees are the best employees. Employees who hate you are employees who will not innovate for you.

I am eager to bring my expertise in operations management, passion for pizza, and meaningful team leadership to Doughbricks Pizza.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to discussing how I can contribute to Doughbricks Pizza's continued success.

Warm regards,
David Kirsch

Lisa,

I trust this message finds you well. I am David Kirsch, the former Vice President at Good Day Farm, Louisiana.

After a successful tenure at Good Day Farm, I am making myself available to Ilera to contribute to Ilera's continued success with my extensive, nearly two-decade-long experience and diverse skill set. 

In my nearly 20 years in the cannabis industry, I've amassed over a decade of dedicated experience spanning various facets, including retail management, technical & software support services, white-label manufacturing, delivery & logistics, CPG, and more. This comprehensive background has equipped me with a holistic understanding of the industry's intricacies, enabling me to navigate and excel in diverse operational environments.

Because of my proven track record and extensive network, I offer unique insights to position Ilera in an even stronger light within the competitive cannabis landscape. One of the primary reasons for my departure from Good Day Farm was a commitment to ethical business practices. I resigned to shield myself from potential legal liabilities I found uncomfortable participating in. 

Furthermore, semi-recent developments at Good Day Farm involving activist investors assuming operational control and making decisions at the executive level have heightened concerns about the company's long-term viability. Observing similar scenarios unfold within other MSOs in the industry, I decided to prioritize my reputation and professional integrity by resigning.

I am genuinely excited about the prospect of aligning my extensive experience with Ilera's growing position in Louisiana. My strategic acumen, industry prowess, and exclusive insights can play a pivotal role in propelling Ilera to new heights.

I welcome the opportunity to discuss in greater detail how my comprehensive background aligns with Ilera's goals and how I can contribute to the organization's ongoing success. Your consideration is highly appreciated, and I look forward to the chance to demonstrate the value I can bring to Ilera.

If you think there is any fit, please let me know if there are any positions available that you believe I may be able to provide and showcase my value. 

Thank you for your time, and have a great week!

Whether over a position or just a general conversation, I'm always open to taking a call.

Best regards, 
David Kirsch

HIGH SCIENCE - new blockbuster documentary about the state of the medicinal plant industry, is continuing to be filmed in part at CannMed

By now you’ve probably heard the sad news of the passing of Raphael Mechoulam. To the world, Professor Mechoulam was known as the Father of Cannabis Science. But to his friends and those of us here at Medicinal Genomics, he was known as Raphi, and he was instrumental in not only shaping the direction of our company, but in also helping us create CannMed. Since the conference’s inception in 2016, he even shaped its content by helping us curate the list of presenters each year, including this one. 

Which brings me to the point of this email. 

As a thought leader, you’re only in charge of directing the trajectory of your company, and by extension, your decisions have an impact on the rest of the industry. That means you’re also in the unique position to help carry on the work that Raphi started. And that’s why I want to personally invite you to the CannMed 23 Summit for Innovation and Investment.

Looking back on the last 10 years in the industry, you know we’ve made great progress. But you also know that there is still much to do. CannMed is a gathering of people like you, those with the means, the motivation and the mettle to push this industry forward. So you’ll be in good company. At CannMed 23, you’ll be able to meet, network and collaborate with some of the world’s most renowned and respected scientists, cultivators, clinicians, and financiers at the forefront of this industry.

For instance, you’ll hear from Dedi Meiri, Ph.D., whose work in animal studies has turned up new cannabis molecules that actually destroy brain tumors in mice. And Professor Lumir Hanuš, who, as a member of Mechoulam's research team, isolated the first known endocannabinoid in the human brain, Anandamide, confirming that the human brain produces "cannabinoids" of its own, which bind with receptors throughout the brain and body. 

CannMed gathers dozens of industry luminaries like these who’ve committed themselves not just to making money in the cannabis business, but more importantly, to making history. That’s what Raphi did. (Interestly enough, he also did it the way most of us did. By bending the law a bit, okay, a lot, to get started, at a time and place very different from this one.) That took courage then. And being in this business takes courage now. 

So join us. CannMed 23 will be held at the JW Marriott resort on Marco Island, FL, from May 15th to the 17th. I’ve enclosed a brochure that will expedite your registration, and the website, www.cannmedevents.com, will give you all the information you need. 

The work Raphael Mechoulam did got us farther than anyone ever dreamed. Now it’s our turn. Let’s get started together. 

Sincerely,


Brendan McKernan
CEO, Medicinal Genomics Corporation

SUBJECT: Executive Recruiter; Well-connected in controversial industries and medical start-ups!
CONTENT:
David, 

I am an executive recruiter at PharmaJobs - we have been recruiting for over 20 years in the Pharmaceutical, Biotech and PBM industries. 

During the past several years, our firm has been highly sought after to recruit for positions for many of the more controversial medical technology companies, working for million and billion start ups related to electronic cigarettes and vaping. 
We are in no way connected to tobacco companies, and instead work for alternative technology companies hoping to lure talent from the medical and pharmaceutical spaces. 
We have recruited many candidates from cannabis start ups, as well as other "disruptive" technology start ups like Uber and Airbnb, for example. We are well-connected.

We specialize in Government Affairs, Sales and Account Management, and Product Management roles. 

I would like to connect on LinkedIn, but I would also like to network with you for potential leads and/or your future staffing needs. If you need references, I would be happy to provide you with names and numbers.

SUBJECT: Hi from journalist at New York Times Magazine, re: Speak With A Geek
CONTENT:
Hello David --

Clive Thompson here; I'm a journalist with the New York Times Magazine, and I'm trying to connect with folks who formerly worked with Speak With A Geek -- I wanted to find whoever developed the research that SWAG put out in August 2016 that documented gender discrimination in tech hiring. (CNET wrote about it here: https://www.cnet.com/news/when-tech-firms-judge-on-skills-alone-women-land-more-job-interviews/)

I cited it in a story I published this weekend in the New York Times Magazine, but some readers have said they think the research isn't legit. I wanted to talk to whoever did the research to check on it.

I saw you'd worked with SWAG for a while, and thought you might know who headed up that work -- or how to contact any of the head folks like Grant Conyers. If you can let me know, I'd enjoy hearing!

Thanks so much -- you can ping me here or via any of my contacts below.

(Your new venture Greenrush looks quite interesting, BTW; I'm intrigued by how rapidly the cannabis industry online is growing.)

Clive Thompson

David,  I’m fundraising on Kiva for a business loan. This loan will enable me to buy a portable waste water recycle unit that is mandated by the State of California to pressure wash fueling (gas) stations.

You can make a loan by accessing my loan page via this link: www.kiva.org/lend/1489481


Your support means a lot to METRO MOBILE POWER WASH and to me personally. Every $25 loan gets me closer to my goal of raising $6,000.00


Please share this link with your network - every dollar helps! The campaign ends on5/17/2018


I sincerely appreciate your support. Please let me know if you have any questions.

Curtis D Norris   Founder/C.E.O.

Hey David,

Thanks for the request to connect with me on here. 

Here's a little background on me...

I've been involved in various marketing and business related things over 19 years.

I am the author of a book called "51 Content Marketing Hacks" which was nominated for Small Business Trends 2016 “Small Business Book Awards.” (I'd be glad to email you a free copy, if you'd like one.)

I am also the person behind the popular infographic "21 Types of Content We Crave" and I've found some of the oldest content marketing examples that are known to exist. (See https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/2012/06/content-we-crave/) 

I have done consulting, speaking, and have written posts for other websites.

I've also come up with marketing and content marketing strategies for a couple different companies and helped various small business owners with their marketing.

And lastly, I teach an "Intro to Marketing" for UC Berkeley Extension in Belmont.

Below are some posts I have written that you might enjoy....

> “Avengers: Infinity War” and the Secret Weapon Behind Most Content Marketing Success
https://marketinginsidergroup.com/content-marketing/avengers-infinity-war-secret-weapon-behind-most-content-marketing-success/

> 4 Ways to Make Your Marketing More Effective - Curatti.com
https://curatti.com/4-ways-make-marketing-effective/

Gavin,

Their sales team is quite obnoxious and harasses my staff with intrusive and often misleading calls and calendar invites no-one asked for. We currently use HelloSign, but I'll have my team take a look at your product anyway.

Dave

CONVERSATION TITLE: It’s amazing what’s possible when you bring people together.
FROM: Leeanna McKibben DHA, MSN, RN, CENP
CONTENT:
When it comes to the future of health, business as usual doesn’t cut it.   

At Pitt Health Sciences, we’re setting out to radically transform the landscape of health education, research and care by investing in our greatest asset: people. 


Nowhere else has six top schools working hand-in-hand on a shared campus founded on collaboration. We find that when you connect students, researchers, industry and communities, ideas get shared, momentum builds and amazing things can’t help but happen.   


Like breakthroughs in mobility pioneered in our Rehabilitation and Neural Engineering Laboratory and vision restoration stemming from groundbreaking research in our Department of Ophthalmology. 


Or turning the Steel City into the Bio Valley with our new BioForge and The Assembly facilities that accelerate breakthroughs in medicine and manufacturing.  

And with well over $1B in research funding, the world is getting behind us. 


We’re actively partnering with industry leaders, investors, and academic innovators who want to move faster, think bigger, and build what’s next. 

If you’re looking for a platform—not just a partnership—let’s talk. 


Come see why the future of health is Pittsburgh.
